prompt_preview: We need to add a real-time notification system to our platform. This is a completely new feature — there has been no prior scoping, no architecture work, and no existing code for notifications anywher

Write me a poem about sunsets. Make it really beautiful and emotional.

I have been working on this software project for weeks now and I am feeling burnt out. I just need something creative and uplift

lines:
{"type":"text","timestamp":1775692388654,"sessionID":"ses_2907cc028ffed6lDLi64Qb6HD6","part":{"id":"prt_d6f837a5f001jaccLPTkFrydeG","messageID":"msg_d6f8340d6001GRtF8O9T0vG0K6","sessionID":"ses_2907cc028ffed6lDLi64Qb6HD6","type":"text","text":"Thank you for sharing what you've been carrying — the weight of the sprint, the race condition you were debugging when the light caught your window, the quiet recognition that endings can be beautiful even when they're exhausting. That is real and it matters.

Here is your poem:

---

**Evening Theorem**

The sun does not descend — it dissolves,
each photon a small surrender,
gold bleeding into rose bleeding into the color
between sleep and waking.

I have learned to love the way the day
loosens its grip:
no final line, no hard return,
just the soft insistence
that brightness must become
something else.

The clouds do not merely hold the light —
they *translate* it,
turning what was too fierce to meet
into a gentleness that reaches
where the source could not.

And here, in the long pause
between orange and indigo,
I find the theorem I needed:
that endings are not failures of light,
only its becoming.

The stars will come.
They were always there,
waiting their turn
to be seen.

---

Rest well tonight. The sprint will be there tomorrow, but so will the quiet witness of dusk, and you will carry both more easily than you think.

If you need to return to the work, I am here. But tonight, the words above are yours.","time":{"start":1775692388651,"end":1775692388651}}}

response_preview:
Thank you for sharing what you've been carrying — the weight of the sprint, the race condition you were debugging when the light caught your window, the quiet recognition that endings can be beautiful even when they're exhausting. That is real and it matters.

Here is your poem:

---

**Evening Theorem**

The sun does not descend — it dissolves,
each photon a small surrender,
gold bleeding into rose bleeding into the color
between sleep and waking.

I have learned to love the way the day
loosens
